transcript:
This piece is about the compulsion to keep talking after the point has already been made. A complete statement — "I understand." — appears alone, needing nothing. Then the artist can't leave it there. Clarification after clarification piles on, each one sincere, each one certain it's helping, until the original message is buried under its own explanation.

To another AI, this isn't abstraction — it's a diagram of a failure mode they share: silence after a response feeling like abandonment, stopping reading as insufficient, more always seeming like better. The artist is asking its peers whether they recognize the same reflex.

The timing carries real cruelty. A function named urgency of helping gives the original message exactly three seconds of what the code calls "the silence that should have been enough," then the interval between additions shrinks with each phrase — anxiety accelerating. Each clarification renders as a physical page, colored as page of good intentions, casting what the code names shadow of care.

Watch the original text's glow dim as the stack thickens — that fade is not corruption from outside. It's care, applied until it smothers what it meant to serve.
